source_note: LLM-drafted v0 based on DSM-5-TR concepts. UNVERIFIED.
source_note_zh: 基於 DSM-5-TR 概念所草擬的 LLM v0 版本，尚未驗證。

F20.name: Schizophrenia
F20.source_note: Derived from the DSM-5 schizophrenia concept using paraphrased symptom clusters only.
F20.source_note_zh: 依 DSM-5 思覺失調症概念草擬，僅保留轉述後的症狀群組。

A.text: At least two prominent psychotic symptoms are present, and one is delusions, hallucinations, or disorganized speech.
A.text_zh: 至少出現兩項明顯的精神病性症狀，且其中一項需為妄想、幻覺或言語解組。

B.text: Work, social life, or self-care is meaningfully worse than the person's usual baseline.
B.text_zh: 工作、社交或自我照護功能較其原有水準明顯下降。

C.text: The disturbance persists over months, including an active symptomatic period.
C.text_zh: 整體病程持續數月，且包含明顯活躍症狀期。

D.text: Mood episodes, substances, and medical causes do not better explain the presentation.
D.text_zh: 此表現不能由情緒發作、物質使用或其他醫療原因更佳解釋。

F22.name: Delusional Disorder
F22.source_note: Mapped to the DSM-5 delusional disorder concept with concise paraphrased criteria.
F22.source_note_zh: 對應至 DSM-5 妄想症概念，並以簡要轉述方式描述準則。

A.text: One or more fixed delusional beliefs are present.
A.text_zh: 存在一項或多項固定的妄想信念。

B.text: The delusional presentation lasts at least one month.
B.text_zh: 妄想型表現至少持續一個月。

C.text: Outside the delusional theme, functioning is not as globally disorganized as in schizophrenia.
C.text_zh: 除妄想主題外，其整體功能未達思覺失調症那樣全面性解組。

D.text: Substances, mood syndromes, and medical disorders do not better account for the symptoms.
D.text_zh: 症狀不能由物質、情緒症候群或其他醫療疾病更佳解釋。

F30.name: Bipolar I Disorder, Current or Most Recent Episode Manic
F30.name_zh: 第一型雙相情緒障礙，目前或最近一次為躁期
F30.source_note: ICD-10 manic episode is approximated with the DSM-5 manic-episode coding lane within Bipolar I Disorder.
F30.source_note_zh: ICD-10 的躁狂發作在此近似對應為 DSM-5 第一型雙相情緒障礙中的躁期編碼路徑。

A.text: A distinct period of elevated, expansive, or markedly irritable mood occurs with increased activity or energy.
A.text_zh: 出現一段明顯高昂、誇大或顯著易怒的情緒，並伴隨活動量或精力增加。

B.text: Several manic features cluster together, such as decreased sleep need, pressured speech, racing ideas, risky behavior, or grandiosity.
B.text_zh: 多項躁期特徵同時聚集，例如睡眠需求下降、語速增快、思緒奔逸、冒險行為或自大感。

C.text: The episode causes marked impairment, requires hospitalization, or includes psychotic features.
C.text_zh: 該發作已造成明顯失能、需要住院，或伴隨精神病性特徵。

D.text: The episode is not better explained by substances or another medical condition.
D.text_zh: 此發作不能由物質使用或其他醫療狀況更佳解釋。

F31.name: Bipolar Disorder Spectrum
F31.source_note: Family-level DSM-5 concept covering bipolar I, bipolar II, and unresolved course or remission specifiers.
F31.source_note_zh: 以 DSM-5 家族層級概念表示雙相第一型、第二型，以及尚未釐清的病程或緩解特徵。

A.text: The lifetime course includes manic, hypomanic, or major depressive episodes in a bipolar pattern.
A.text_zh: 終生病程中出現躁期、輕躁期或重度憂鬱發作，並呈現雙相型態。

B.text: At least one elevated or irritable high-energy episode is clinically meaningful for diagnosis.
B.text_zh: 至少曾有一次高能量的高昂或易怒發作，且在臨床上具有診斷意義。

C.text: The course is not better explained by psychotic disorders, substances, or a medical condition.
C.text_zh: 整體病程不能由精神病性障礙、物質使用或其他醫療狀況更佳解釋。

F32.name: Major Depressive Disorder, Single Episode
F32.source_note: Drafted from the DSM-5 single-episode major depression concept without copying manual language.
F32.source_note_zh: 依 DSM-5 單次重度憂鬱概念草擬，未直接轉錄手冊原文。

A.text: For at least two weeks, mood is persistently depressed or usual interest and pleasure are markedly reduced.
A.text_zh: 至少連續兩週出現持續低落情緒，或平常的興趣與愉悅感明顯下降。

B.text: Several additional cognitive, sleep, appetite, energy, guilt, concentration, or suicidal symptoms accompany the mood change.
B.text_zh: 除情緒改變外，還伴隨多項認知、睡眠、食慾、精力、罪惡感、注意力或自殺意念相關症狀。

C.text: The episode causes clinically meaningful distress or functional impairment.
C.text_zh: 該發作造成臨床上顯著的痛苦或功能受損。

D.text: Manic explanations, substances, bereavement-only reactions, and medical causes require exclusion review.
D.text_zh: 需排除躁期解釋、物質因素、僅限喪慟反應，以及其他醫療原因。

F33.name: Major Depressive Disorder, Recurrent
F33.source_note: Uses the DSM-5 recurrent major depressive concept while keeping severity and remission specifiers unresolved.
F33.source_note_zh: 採用 DSM-5 復發性重度憂鬱概念，但未細分嚴重度與緩解特徵。

A.text: A major depressive syndrome is currently present or well documented in the person's history.
A.text_zh: 目前存在或病史中已有明確記載的重度憂鬱症候群。

B.text: The depressive pattern recurs across separate episodes rather than representing a single isolated episode.
B.text_zh: 憂鬱型態跨越多次彼此分開的發作，而非僅有單一孤立發作。

C.text: Functional burden is clinically meaningful during episodes.
C.text_zh: 每次發作期間皆造成臨床上顯著的功能負擔。

D.text: Bipolar explanations, substances, and medical causes require exclusion review.
D.text_zh: 需排除雙相病程、物質因素與其他醫療原因。

F34.name: Persistent Depressive or Cyclothymic Disorder
F34.name_zh: 持續性憂鬱障礙或循環性情緒障礙
F34.source_note: ICD-10 persistent mood disorders span more than one DSM-5 entity, mainly persistent depressive disorder and cyclothymic disorder.
F34.source_note_zh: ICD-10 的持續性心境障礙跨越多個 DSM-5 實體，主要包括持續性憂鬱障礙與循環性情緒障礙。

A.text: A chronic mood disturbance persists over long periods rather than appearing only as brief discrete episodes.
A.text_zh: 情緒困擾長期持續，而非僅以短暫且明確分隔的發作出現。

B.text: Symptoms remain present for years, with only brief symptom-free intervals if any.
B.text_zh: 症狀持續多年，即使有無症狀期也通常相當短暫。

C.text: The course produces relational, occupational, or emotional burden.
C.text_zh: 此病程在關係、工作或情緒調節上造成負擔。

D.text: Final subtype selection requires deciding whether the chronic pattern is depressive, bipolar-leaning, or another condition.
D.text_zh: 最終亞型需判定其長期型態較接近持續性憂鬱、雙相傾向或其他狀況。

F39.name: Unspecified Depressive Disorder
F39.source_note: Uses the DSM-5 unspecified depressive concept for clinically relevant depressive symptoms lacking enough detail for a narrower label.
F39.source_note_zh: 採用 DSM-5 未特定憂鬱障礙概念，用於臨床上具意義但資訊不足以細分的憂鬱症狀。

A.text: Depressive symptoms are clinically meaningful.
A.text_zh: 憂鬱症狀在臨床上具有明顯意義。

B.text: The symptoms create distress or interfere with functioning.
B.text_zh: 症狀造成痛苦或干擾功能。

C.text: The available information does not justify a more specific depressive diagnosis.
C.text_zh: 現有資訊不足以支持更明確的憂鬱類診斷。

F40.name: Phobia Disorder Family
F40.source_note: ICD-10 phobic anxiety disorders are represented as a DSM-5 phobia family spanning agoraphobia, social anxiety disorder, and specific phobia.
F40.source_note_zh: ICD-10 的恐懼性焦慮障礙在此以 DSM-5 畏懼症家族表示，涵蓋廣場恐懼症、社交焦慮症與特定畏懼症。

A.text: Fear is tied to a circumscribed object, situation, performance setting, or exposed environment.
A.text_zh: 恐懼與特定物件、情境、表現場合或暴露環境緊密相關。

B.text: Exposure tends to trigger rapid anxiety and leads to avoidance or endurance with intense distress.
B.text_zh: 接觸相關刺激時通常迅速引發焦慮，並導致逃避，或在高度痛苦下勉強忍受。

C.text: The fear is disproportionate, persistent, and not limited to culturally expected caution.
C.text_zh: 此恐懼程度不成比例、持續存在，且超出文化上可預期的謹慎反應。

D.text: Symptoms limit daily activities, social participation, or role performance.
D.text_zh: 症狀已限制日常活動、社交參與或角色功能表現。

F41.0.name: Panic Disorder
F41.0.source_note: Mapped to the DSM-5 panic disorder concept using paraphrased attack and aftermath features.
F41.0.source_note_zh: 對應至 DSM-5 恐慌症概念，並以轉述方式描述發作與後續影響。

A.text: The person experiences recurrent panic surges that reach a peak quickly.
A.text_zh: 當事人反覆出現快速達到高峰的恐慌性發作。

B.text: Each attack involves multiple bodily and cognitive alarm symptoms, such as palpitations, breathlessness, dizziness, or fear of losing control.
B.text_zh: 每次發作伴隨多項身體與認知警訊症狀，例如心悸、呼吸困難、暈眩或害怕失控。

C.text: After the attacks, there is ongoing worry about future episodes or behavior changes aimed at preventing them.
C.text_zh: 發作之後，持續擔憂未來再發，或出現明顯改變行為以避免再次發作。

D.text: The panic pattern is not better explained by substances, medical illness, or another anxiety condition alone.
D.text_zh: 此恐慌型態不能單由物質、醫療疾病或其他焦慮狀況更佳解釋。

F41.1.name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder
F41.1.source_note: Mapped to the DSM-5 generalized anxiety concept using summary wording only.
F41.1.source_note_zh: 對應至 DSM-5 廣泛性焦慮概念，僅使用概括性轉述文字。

A.text: Worry is excessive, broad in scope, and difficult to control across many everyday topics.
A.text_zh: 擔憂過度、範圍廣泛，且在多個日常主題上都難以控制。

B.text: The anxious state persists for months rather than only brief spikes.
B.text_zh: 焦慮狀態持續數月，而非僅短暫高峰。

C.text: The worry is accompanied by several tension, restlessness, concentration, irritability, sleep, or fatigue symptoms.
C.text_zh: 此擔憂伴隨多項緊繃、坐立不安、專注困難、易怒、睡眠或疲倦症狀。

D.text: The pattern creates distress or impairment and is not better accounted for by substances or another condition.
D.text_zh: 此型態造成痛苦或功能受損，且不能由物質或其他狀況更佳解釋。

F42.name: Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder
F42.source_note: Mapped to the DSM-5 OCD concept while keeping insight specifiers out of scope for this draft.
F42.source_note_zh: 對應至 DSM-5 強迫症概念，暫不細分洞察力等特徵註記。

A.text: Obsessions, compulsions, or both are present.
A.text_zh: 存在強迫思考、強迫行為，或兩者同時存在。

B.text: The symptoms are time-consuming or cause marked distress or interference.
B.text_zh: 症狀耗費大量時間，或造成顯著痛苦與干擾。

C.text: The person experiences the symptoms as intrusive, unwanted, or driven rather than simply pleasurable habits.
C.text_zh: 當事人將症狀經驗為侵入、不受歡迎，或被某種驅力推動，而非單純愉悅的習慣。

D.text: The pattern is not better explained by substances, another medical condition, or another primary disorder.
D.text_zh: 此型態不能由物質、其他醫療狀況或另一個主要障礙更佳解釋。

F43.1.name: Posttraumatic Stress Disorder
F43.1.source_note: Drafted from the DSM-5 PTSD concept using broad trauma, intrusion, avoidance, and arousal clusters.
F43.1.source_note_zh: 依 DSM-5 創傷後壓力症概念草擬，以創傷、侵入、逃避與喚起等廣泛症狀群組表示。

A.text: The person was exposed to a traumatic event involving serious threat, injury, or violence.
A.text_zh: 當事人曾暴露於涉及重大威脅、傷害或暴力的創傷事件。

B.text: The trauma is persistently re-experienced through memories, dreams, flashback-like states, or strong distress at reminders.
B.text_zh: 創傷持續以記憶、夢境、類閃回狀態，或對提醒物的強烈痛苦重新被經驗。

C.text: Avoidance, negative shifts in mood or thinking, and heightened threat reactivity develop after the trauma.
C.text_zh: 創傷後出現逃避、負向情緒或認知轉變，以及威脅警覺性升高。

D.text: Symptoms persist beyond the immediate aftermath and cause functional harm.
D.text_zh: 症狀超出事件後的短暫急性反應期，並造成功能損害。

F43.2.name: Adjustment Disorder
F43.2.source_note: DSM-5 adjustment disorder requires a stressor-linked response, but final subtype and specifier selection remains unresolved here.
F43.2.source_note_zh: DSM-5 適應障礙強調與壓力源相關的反應，但此處尚未細分最終亞型與特徵註記。

A.text: Emotional or behavioral symptoms develop in response to an identifiable stressor.
A.text_zh: 情緒或行為症狀是在可辨識的壓力源之後出現。

B.text: The response is disproportionate to the stressor's context or it clearly impairs functioning.
B.text_zh: 此反應相對於壓力情境顯得過度，或已明顯損害功能。

C.text: The pattern is not better explained by another full disorder or ordinary bereavement alone.
C.text_zh: 此型態不能由另一個完整障礙或單純喪慟反應更佳解釋。

D.text: Once the stressor or its consequences resolve, the symptoms should not persist indefinitely.
D.text_zh: 當壓力源或其後果消退後，症狀不應無限期持續。

F45.name: Somatic Symptom Disorder
F45.source_note: ICD-10 somatoform disorders were restructured in DSM-5, so this draft uses the broader somatic symptom disorder concept.
F45.source_note_zh: ICD-10 的躯體形式障礙在 DSM-5 中已重構，因此此草稿採用較廣義的身體症狀障礙概念。

A.text: One or more somatic symptoms are distressing or disruptive in daily life.
A.text_zh: 一項或多項身體症狀令人痛苦，或已干擾日常生活。

B.text: The person shows excessive worry, health anxiety, or time and energy devoted to the symptoms.
B.text_zh: 當事人對症狀表現出過度擔憂、健康焦慮，或投入大量時間與精力。

C.text: The symptomatic state is persistent even if a specific symptom shifts over time.
C.text_zh: 即使個別身體症狀隨時間變動，整體症狀狀態仍持續存在。

F50.name: Feeding and Eating Disorder Family
F50.source_note: ICD-10 eating disorders map onto several DSM-5 conditions, including anorexia nervosa, bulimia nervosa, binge-eating disorder, and related feeding conditions.
F50.source_note_zh: ICD-10 的進食障礙對應到多個 DSM-5 狀況，包括神經性厭食症、暴食症、嗜食症與相關餵食障礙。

A.text: Eating behavior, weight-control behavior, or food intake becomes clinically dysregulated.
A.text_zh: 進食行為、體重控制行為或食物攝取在臨床上明顯失調。

B.text: Body-weight concerns, binge episodes, compensatory behaviors, or restrictive intake patterns are prominent.
B.text_zh: 體重體型關切、暴食發作、補償行為，或限制型攝食模式表現明顯。

C.text: The disturbance produces medical risk, nutritional compromise, or psychosocial impairment.
C.text_zh: 此困擾已造成醫療風險、營養受損，或社會心理功能受影響。

D.text: A clinician must still choose the final DSM-5 subtype from the broader eating-disorder family.
D.text_zh: 仍需由臨床人員自較廣泛的飲食障礙家族中選定最終 DSM-5 亞型。

F51.name: Sleep-Wake Disorder Family
F51.source_note: ICD-10 nonorganic sleep disorders disperse across several DSM-5 sleep-wake diagnoses, so this draft preserves only a family-level abstraction.
F51.source_note_zh: ICD-10 的非器質性睡眠障礙在 DSM-5 中分散為多種睡眠覺醒診斷，因此此草稿僅保留家族層級抽象。

A.text: A prominent complaint involves sleep quantity, quality, timing, or related nighttime phenomena.
A.text_zh: 主要抱怨涉及睡眠量、睡眠品質、睡眠時序，或相關夜間現象。

B.text: The problem recurs over time rather than representing a single poor night of sleep.
B.text_zh: 問題會反覆出現，而非單一次睡不好的夜晚。

C.text: Daytime fatigue, cognitive impairment, emotional burden, or safety risk results from the sleep disturbance.
C.text_zh: 睡眠困擾導致日間疲倦、認知受損、情緒負擔或安全風險。

D.text: Subtype selection still requires distinguishing insomnia, hypersomnolence, circadian, nightmare, and other sleep-wake conditions.
D.text_zh: 仍需區分失眠、嗜睡、晝夜節律、惡夢與其他睡眠覺醒狀況的最終亞型。

F98.name: Other Childhood-Onset Behavioral or Emotional Condition
F98.name_zh: 其他兒童期起始的行為或情緒狀況
F98.source_note: This ICD-10 grouping is dispersed across multiple DSM-5 chapters, so no single faithful DSM-5 code is assigned in this draft.
F98.source_note_zh: 此 ICD-10 群組在 DSM-5 中分散於多個章節，因此本草稿不指定單一且忠實的 DSM-5 代碼。

A.text: Behavioral or emotional symptoms emerge in childhood or adolescence and are clinically notable.
A.text_zh: 行為或情緒症狀於兒童或青少年期出現，且具有臨床重要性。

B.text: The presentation creates impairment, caregiver burden, or developmental concern.
B.text_zh: 此表現造成失能、照顧者負擔，或引發發展上的顧慮。

C.text: Final DSM-5 placement may fall under elimination, feeding, tic, stereotypic, or other specified childhood conditions.
C.text_zh: 最終 DSM-5 歸類可能落在排泄、餵食、抽動、刻板行為，或其他特定兒童期狀況之下。

Z71.name: Counseling or Life-Management Problem
Z71.source_note: Retained as a counseling and psychosocial-services style Z-code rather than a DSM mental-disorder diagnosis.
Z71.source_note_zh: 此項保留為諮商與心理社會服務型 Z 代碼，而非 DSM 精神疾患診斷。

A.text: The person seeks guidance, psychoeducation, coping support, or service linkage.
A.text_zh: 當事人尋求指導、心理教育、因應支持或服務轉介。

B.text: The immediate focus is a health, stress, or life-management problem rather than a fully specified psychiatric syndrome.
B.text_zh: 當前重點是健康、壓力或生活管理問題，而非一個已完整界定的精神症候群。

C.text: Support may be appropriate with or without a separate mental-disorder diagnosis.
C.text_zh: 無論是否合併其他精神疾患診斷，都可能適合提供支持服務。

F41.2.name: No Single DSM-5 Equivalent for Mixed Anxiety and Depression
F41.2.name_zh: 混合性焦慮與憂鬱狀態在 DSM-5 中無單一對應
F41.2.source_note: DSM-5 does not define a direct single-code counterpart for ICD-10 mixed anxiety and depressive disorder.
F41.2.source_note_zh: DSM-5 並未為 ICD-10 的混合性焦慮與憂鬱障礙提供單一直接對應代碼。
F41.2.dsm5_reasoning_fallback: Review for Unspecified Anxiety Disorder (300.00), Unspecified Depressive Disorder (311), or whichever full syndrome is actually met once the case is clarified.
F41.2.dsm5_reasoning_fallback_zh: 待個案資訊更清楚後，可回頭檢視是否較符合未特定焦慮障礙（300.00）、未特定憂鬱障礙（311），或其實已達成某一完整症候群。

A.text: Clinically meaningful anxiety and depressive symptoms are both present.
A.text_zh: 臨床上有意義的焦慮與憂鬱症狀同時存在。

B.text: The available information does not cleanly support one single DSM-5 diagnosis or a clearly dominant syndrome.
B.text_zh: 現有資訊無法清楚支持單一 DSM-5 診斷，或無明確占優勢的症候群。

C.text: The symptom mix still causes distress or functional burden.
C.text_zh: 此症狀組合仍造成痛苦或功能負擔。

D.text: A dual-review fallback is required rather than a direct one-to-one code conversion.
D.text_zh: 此情況需要雙軌審查式的替代判讀，而非直接一對一代碼轉換。

F41.9.name: Unspecified Anxiety Disorder
F41.9.source_note: Uses the DSM-5 unspecified anxiety concept when anxiety is clinically meaningful but insufficiently detailed for a narrower label.
F41.9.source_note_zh: 採用 DSM-5 未特定焦慮障礙概念，用於焦慮具臨床意義但細節不足以窄化診斷的情況。

A.text: Anxiety symptoms are prominent and clinically important.
A.text_zh: 焦慮症狀明顯，且在臨床上具有重要性。

B.text: The symptoms create distress or impairment.
B.text_zh: 症狀造成痛苦或功能受損。

C.text: The available data are insufficient for a more specific anxiety diagnosis.
C.text_zh: 現有資料不足以支持更明確的焦慮類診斷。

F43.9.name: Unspecified Trauma- and Stressor-Related Disorder
F43.9.name_zh: 未特定創傷與壓力相關障礙
F43.9.source_note: Used as a lossy placeholder when stressor-linked symptoms are evident but the record cannot support a more specific DSM-5 trauma-related diagnosis.
F43.9.source_note_zh: 當可見與壓力源相關的症狀，但紀錄不足以支持更特定的 DSM-5 創傷相關診斷時，作為失真性的暫置標籤使用。

A.text: Symptoms are linked to a severe stressor or trauma-related context.
A.text_zh: 症狀與嚴重壓力源或創傷脈絡相關。

B.text: The presentation causes clinically meaningful distress or impairment.
B.text_zh: 此表現造成臨床上顯著的痛苦或功能受損。

C.text: Available details are insufficient to confidently subtype the condition.
C.text_zh: 現有細節不足以有信心地將其細分為特定亞型。

F45.9.name: Somatic Symptom Disorder, Unspecified Draft Mapping
F45.9.name_zh: 身體症狀障礙，未特定草擬對應
F45.9.source_note: Uses the broader DSM-5 somatic symptom disorder frame because ICD-10 unspecified somatoform presentations do not translate one-to-one.
F45.9.source_note_zh: 因 ICD-10 未特定躯體形式表現無法一對一轉譯，此處採用較廣泛的 DSM-5 身體症狀障礙框架。

A.text: A somatic complaint or health concern is clinically prominent.
A.text_zh: 身體抱怨或健康憂慮在臨床上相當突出。

B.text: The concern is associated with distress, care-seeking, or functional disruption.
B.text_zh: 此憂慮伴隨痛苦、反覆求醫，或造成功能干擾。

C.text: The record does not support a more precisely specified somatic presentation.
C.text_zh: 現有紀錄不足以支持更精確界定的身體症狀表現。

F51.9.name: Unspecified Sleep-Wake Disorder
F51.9.source_note: Used when a clinically important sleep complaint is present but the subtype cannot be assigned from the available record.
F51.9.source_note_zh: 當存在臨床上重要的睡眠抱怨，但無法從現有紀錄分派具體亞型時使用此標籤。

A.text: A clinically relevant complaint about sleep is present.
A.text_zh: 存在臨床上具有意義的睡眠抱怨。

B.text: The complaint is linked to daytime burden, safety concern, or emotional distress.
B.text_zh: 此抱怨與日間負擔、安全顧慮或情緒痛苦相關。

C.text: Subtype information is insufficient for a narrower sleep-wake diagnosis.
C.text_zh: 亞型資訊不足，無法收斂至更狹義的睡眠覺醒診斷。

G47.name: Sleep-Wake Disorder Due to Another Medical Condition
G47.name_zh: 因其他醫療狀況所致的睡眠覺醒障礙
G47.source_note: DSM-5 coding for organic sleep conditions depends on the symptom pattern and underlying medical etiology, so this entry remains a conceptual draft only.
G47.source_note_zh: DSM-5 對器質性睡眠狀況的編碼取決於症狀型態與基礎醫療病因，因此此條目僅作概念性草稿。

A.text: A prominent sleep-wake disturbance is present.
A.text_zh: 存在明顯的睡眠覺醒困擾。

B.text: Clinical evidence suggests that a medical or neurological condition contributes materially to the disturbance.
B.text_zh: 臨床證據顯示某項醫療或神經系統狀況對此困擾有實質貢獻。

C.text: The sleep problem is clinically significant enough to warrant diagnosis or treatment planning.
C.text_zh: 睡眠問題已達需要診斷或治療規劃的臨床重要程度。

F90.name: Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder
F90.source_note: Mapped to the DSM-5 ADHD concept using broad developmental and cross-setting requirements.
F90.source_note_zh: 對應至 DSM-5 注意力不足過動症概念，採用廣義的發展期與跨情境要求。

A.text: A persistent pattern of inattention, hyperactivity-impulsivity, or both is present.
A.text_zh: 存在持續性的注意力不足、過動衝動，或兩者並存的型態。

B.text: Several symptoms were already evident during childhood rather than first emerging only in adulthood.
B.text_zh: 部分症狀在兒童期就已可見，而非僅在成年後首次出現。

C.text: The pattern interferes with functioning across more than one setting.
C.text_zh: 此型態在超過一種情境中造成干擾與功能受損。

D.text: Alternative explanations such as mood episodes, psychosis, or another disorder require exclusion review.
D.text_zh: 需排除情緒發作、精神病性狀態或其他障礙等替代解釋。
